title: podocarpus elatus
intro: Podocarpus elatus, also known as the fern pine or yellow tree, is a large evergreen coniferous tree native to Australia and New Guinea. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The leaves and bark of the Podocarpus elatus plant have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although the use of these preparations should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications.
